entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Short-duration cultivars of pigeonpea used to study the effects of drought stress

entities.name: DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: Water stress applied at specific growth stages to evaluate its impact on yield and phenology

entities.name: STRESS 1
entities.description: Drought stress applied during the late vegetative and flowering stages

entities.name: STRESS 2
entities.description: Drought stress applied during the flowering and early pod development stages

entities.name: STRESS 3
entities.description: Drought stress applied during the pod fill stage

entities.name: ICRISAT CENTRE, INDIA
entities.description: The location where the field experiments were conducted

entities.name: TIME TO FLOWERING
entities.description: The duration from planting to the onset of flowering

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The total weight of seeds produced per unit area

entities.name: HARVEST INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of seed yield to total dry matter production

entities.name: TOTAL DRY MATTER PRODUCTION
entities.description: The total biomass accumulated by the plant

entities.name: NUMBER OF PODS PER SQUARE METER
entities.description: A yield component measuring the density of pods per unit area

entities.name: DROUGHT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the plant to maintain seed yield under drought conditions

entities.name: FLOWERING SYNCHRONIZATION
entities.description: The degree of uniformity in the timing of flowering across the plant

entities.name: POD SIZE
entities.description: The physical dimensions of the seed pods

entities.name: SEEDS PER POD
entities.description: The number of seeds contained within a single pod

entities.name: PHENOLOGY
entities.description: The timing of biological growth stages in the plant

entities.name: DETERMINATE AND INDETERMINATE CULTIVARS
entities.description: Classification of cultivars based on their growth habit

relationships.target: NUMBER OF PODS PER SQUARE METER
relationships.description: Only yield component significantly affected by water stress
Defined as the stability of seed yield under drought conditions
Ability to maintain TDM may improve drought resistance
Low flowering synchronization may improve drought resistance
Small pod size may improve drought resistance
Few seeds per pod may improve drought resistance
Large 100-seed weight may improve drought resistance
Study focused on the effects of drought stress on its phenology
No consistent differences found in ability to maintain yield under stress 1 and 2
